short_description: Open-source LLMNR/NBT-NS/mDNS poisoner and rogue authentication servers used to coerce and capture Windows network credentials and facilitate NTLM relay in Active Directory environments.
overview_notes: Responder is a widely used assessment tool that listens for broadcast/multicast name-resolution traffic (LLMNR, NetBIOS Name Service, and often mDNS) on local subnets, forges responses to misdirect clients to the attacker's host, and then presents rogue services (for example SMB/HTTP/MSSQL/LDAP/FTP) to trigger NTLM challenge–response authentication. The captured Net-NTLM credentials can be cracked offline or relayed to other services (commonly via impacket's ntlmrelayx) when signing protections are not enforced. Mitigations documented by vendors include disabling LLMNR/NBT-NS, enforcing SMB/LDAP signing, and enabling protections like Extended Protection for Authentication (EPA).
what_it_is_notes:
- A poisoner for Windows name-resolution fallbacks (LLMNR, NBT-NS; commonly also mDNS) that replies to queries for non-existent or mis-typed hosts.
- A suite of lightweight rogue servers that solicit NTLM authentication from victims and log captured challenge–response material.
- A red-team and penetration-testing utility referenced in MITRE ATT&CK T1557.001 (LLMNR/NBT-NS Poisoning and SMB Relay) and in defensive advisories as a representative tool for this attack class.
how_it_works_notes:
- Name-resolution poisoning: When a client cannot resolve a hostname via DNS, it may broadcast/multicast LLMNR/NBT-NS (and sometimes mDNS). Responder forges authoritative replies, mapping the sought name to the attacker's address.
- Rogue service interaction: The tool exposes emulated services (for example SMB/HTTP/MSSQL/LDAP/FTP/POP3/IMAP/SMTP, WPAD) so that the coerced client initiates NTLM authentication to the attacker-controlled host.
- Credential capture/relay: The tool records Net-NTLMv1/v2 challenge–response exchanges. These can be used for offline password cracking or relayed to other protocols lacking enforced signing or channel binding, typically using a separate relay tool (for example ntlmrelayx).
inputs: Broadcast/multicast name-resolution queries (LLMNR, NBT-NS; sometimes mDNS) from Windows or other hosts
Victim-initiated connections to attacker-hosted rogue services (SMB, HTTP, MSSQL, LDAP, etc.)
Environmental misconfigurations (LLMNR/NBT-NS enabled; NTLM allowed; signing/channel binding not enforced)
Optional coercion traffic from separate techniques (for example PrinterBug, PetitPotam, DFSCoerce) that force authentication attempts
outputs: Captured Net-NTLMv1/Net-NTLMv2 challenge–response hashes and related metadata (source IP/hostname, timestamp, queried name)
Authentication transcripts to rogue services (for example SMB/HTTP)
Potential downstream authentication on relayed targets (when paired with relay tooling)
Network/host telemetry side-effects (for example unusual name-resolution responses; IDS/EDR alerts; Windows logon events on relayed targets)
core_concepts: Mitigations: disable LLMNR/NBT-NS, enforce SMB/LDAP signing, EPA/channel binding, limit or disable NTLM
typical_workflow: Phase 1 – Coercion/Poisoning: Wait for or trigger LLMNR/NBT-NS/mDNS queries; forge replies that point victims to the attacker.
Phase 2 – Interception: Host rogue services so the victim initiates NTLM authentication to the attacker; record challenge–response material.
Phase 3 – Relay/Use: Optionally relay captured NTLM to other services lacking enforced signing/channel binding, or crack offline; validate exposure and detections in a controlled assessment.
use_cases: Security assessments of Active Directory environments to identify exposure to LLMNR/NBT-NS poisoning and NTLM relay (ATT&CK T1557.001)
Purple-team exercises to validate controls such as disabling LLMNR/NBT-NS, enforcing SMB/LDAP signing, and enabling EPA/channel binding
Threat hunting and detection engineering for broadcast name-resolution poisoning and rogue WPAD/SMB/HTTP servers
Investigation of authentication anomalies where credentials are used from unexpected hosts due to relay
Exposure reviews tied to coercion techniques (for example PrinterBug, PetitPotam, DFSCoerce) that precede NTLM relay
limitations: Effectiveness drops when organizations disable LLMNR/NBT-NS and mDNS or implement AD DNS hygiene.
NTLM relay is blocked where SMB/LDAP signing, TLS channel binding, or EPA are enforced; NTLMv1 disabled reduces cracking value.
Rogue DHCP/WPAD or aggressive modes can disrupt user experience or network services and are often flagged by IDS/EDR/NDR.
Modern defensive content specifically detects Responder-like traffic patterns and rogue service fingerprints.
Does not bypass protocol-level protections such as Kerberos with mutual auth, SMB encryption, or strict HTTPS with valid cert pinning.

related_tools.name: impacket ntlmrelayx
related_tools.relationship: Commonly paired to forward captured NTLM auth to SMB/LDAP/HTTP/MSSQL when signing/channel binding are not enforced.

related_tools.name: Inveigh
related_tools.relationship: PowerShell/.NET alternative for LLMNR/NBT-NS/mDNS spoofing and credential capture on Windows.

related_tools.name: Pretender
related_tools.relationship: Modern LLMNR/NBNS/mDNS spoofing tool; functionally adjacent to Responder.

related_tools.name: mitm6
related_tools.relationship: Abuses IPv6/DHCPv6 to become DNS server and coerce authentication; complementary coercion vector when LLMNR/NBT-NS are disabled.

related_tools.name: PetitPotam (EFSRPC)
related_tools.relationship: Coercion technique that can force NTLM authentication; often used before relay; not a poisoner itself.

related_tools.name: PrinterBug/SpoolSample
related_tools.relationship: Coercion of authentication via Spooler service; often used to feed relay workflows.

related_tools.name: CrackMapExec
related_tools.relationship: Operational framework that integrates credential capture/relay workflows in AD testing.

related_tools.name: Detect-Responder (osquery extension)
related_tools.relationship: Defensive detector for active Responder or spoofers on the network.

related_tools.name: VindicateTool
related_tools.relationship: Defensive sensor for detecting LLMNR/NBNS/mDNS spoofing including Responder.

evidence_gaps: The primary GitHub repository (lgandx/Responder) was referenced widely, but the main landing page could not be retrieved during this collection; exact current version history and maintainership status as of April 7, 2026 are not confirmed here.

The precise list of built-in rogue servers and optional modules can vary by release; sources consistently mention SMB/HTTP/MSSQL/LDAP and others, but an authoritative, up-to-date module matrix was not accessible in this crawl.

sources.title: MITRE ATT&CK T1557.001: LLMNR/NBT-NS Poisoning and SMB Relay
KB5005413: Mitigating NTLM Relay Attacks on AD CS (includes PetitPotam context and mitigations)
PREVENT-12: Notes on SMB signing and relay limitations
CISA RVA Advisory (references LLMNR/NBT-NS/mDNS poisoning and tooling)
LLMNR/NBT-NS Poisoning and SMB Relay (context and mentions of Responder)
